---
title: "Investors helper"
author: "Adam Baranowski, Mateusz Wilk"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    logo: www/logo.svg
    css: styles.css
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggsci)
library(DT)

source("bonds_vs_stocks.R")
```

About / Help {icon="fa-info-circle"}
=======================================================================

Column {style="overflow-y: auto;"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Market Simulation Engine Guide*

#### *Overview & Purpose*
This dashboard is an educational simulator designed to illustrate the mechanics of long-term compounding, market volatility, and the psychological impact of risk. It is *not* a deterministic financial planning tool or formalized financial advice. By exploring different asset allocations and macroeconomic settings, you can build an intuition for how extreme market events shape terminal wealth.

#### *How to Use the Dashboard*
1. Navigate to the *Portfolio* tab.
2. Adjust the *Global Parameters* to match your time horizon and risk profile.
3. Configure *Strategy #1* and optionally compare it with *Strategy #2*.
4. Open *Goal Planner*, choose a target purchasing-power multiple, and compare which allocations are most likely to reach it. Select a table row to inspect that allocation's outcomes.

---

#### *The Engine: Stocks vs. Bonds*
For each strategy or allocation, the model evaluates 999 percentile-based market outcomes from 0.1% to 99.9%. Historical data supplies the growth, volatility, inflation, and bond-yield parameters; the displayed outcomes are deterministic distribution scenarios rather than replayed historical periods or random Monte Carlo draws.

* *Equities (Stocks):* Modeled using historical real growth and volatility. A t-distribution represents fat-tailed boom and bust outcomes.
* *Fixed Income (Bonds):* Treated as the stabilizing asset, growing at a constant rate derived from historical real 10-year Treasury yields.
* *The Trade-off:* Increasing stock allocation raises potential wealth while widening uncertainty and downside exposure.

#### *Data Sources*
The model can estimate parameters from Vanguard 500 Index Fund Investor Shares (`VFINX`), the FRED Consumer Price Index (`CPIAUCSL`), and the FRED 10-Year Treasury yield (`GS10`). Normal dashboard startup uses bundled baseline parameters so interaction does not wait for external providers. Run `refresh_market_parameter_cache()` manually to refresh the local cache from those sources.

---

#### *The Math of Psychology: Risk Aversion ($\gamma$)*
The dashboard evaluates final wealth using *Constant Relative Risk Aversion (CRRA)* utility:

$$U(W) = \frac{W^{1-\gamma}}{1-\gamma}$$

When $\gamma = 1$, the function transitions to $U(W) = \ln(W)$.

#### *The Gamma Profile Scale*

* *$\gamma = 0$:* Risk-agnostic; prioritizes the mathematical average.
* *$\gamma = 1$:* Risk-tolerant; accepts large drawdowns for upside.
* *$\gamma = 2$:* High capacity; prioritizes growth with some stability.
* *$\gamma = 3$:* Average investor balance.
* *$\gamma = 4$:* Lower capacity; wealth preservation is more important.
* *$\gamma = 5-6$:* Fragile or near zero-loss situation.

<div style="text-align: right; margin-bottom: 20px;">
  <img src="www/PP_logo.png" alt="University Logo" style="max-width: 300px;">
</div>

Portfolio {icon="fa-chart-line"}
=======================================================================

<style>
  .form-group, .shiny-input-container { margin-bottom: 0 !important; padding-bottom: 0 !important; }
  .irs { margin-top: -5px !important; }
  .irs-bottom { height: 0 !important; }
  .irs-grid { display: none; }
  h4 { margin-top: 5px !important; margin-bottom: 5px !important; }
  .checkbox { margin-top: 0 !important; margin-bottom: 0 !important; }
</style>

Row {data-height=200}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Global Parameters* {data-width=400}

```{r ui_global_params}
fluidRow(
  column(
    3,
    div(
      style = "text-align: right; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #2c3e50;",
      "Time Horizon (Years):"
    )
  ),
  column(
    9,
    sliderInput(
      "time_horizon",
      NULL,
      min = 1,
      max = 50,
      value = 30,
      step = 1,
      width = "100%"
    )
  )
)

risk_slider <- sliderInput(
  "risk_aversion",
  NULL,
  min = 0,
  max = 6,
  value = 3,
  step = 0.25,
  width = "100%"
)
risk_slider$children[[2]]$attribs$`data-grid-num` <- 6

fluidRow(
  style = "margin-top: 15px;",
  column(
    3,
    div(
      style = "text-align: right; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #2c3e50;",
      "Risk Aversion (Gamma):",
      div(
        style = "font-size: 0.8em; color: #7f8c8d; font-weight: normal;",
        renderText({
          g <- input$risk_aversion
          if (g < 1) return("[Risk Agnostic]")
          if (g < 2) return("[Surplus / Play Money]")
          if (g < 2.5) return("[High Capacity / Young]")
          if (g <= 3) return("[Average Investor]")
          if (g <= 4) return("[Low Capacity / Older]")
          if (g <= 5) return("[Fragile Situation]")
          "[Zero-Loss Mindset]"
        })
      )
    )
  ),
  column(9, risk_slider)
)
```

### *Strategy Configuration* {data-width=500}

```{r ui_strategy_config}
tagList(
  div(
    style = "margin-bottom: 0px;",
    fluidRow(
      column(
        3,
        h4(HTML(paste0(
          "<span style='color:", color_wealth_strat1,
          ";'>&#9632;</span> <b>Strategy #1</b>"
        )))
      ),
      column(
        2,
        div(
          style = "text-align: right; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #7f8c8d;",
          renderText({ paste0("Bonds: ", 100 - input$strat1_stocks, "%") })
        )
      ),
      column(
        5,
        sliderInput(
          "strat1_stocks",
          NULL,
          min = 0,
          max = 100,
          value = 90,
          post = "%",
          width = "100%"
        )
      ),
      column(
        2,
        div(
          style = "text-align: left; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #2c3e50;",
          renderText({ paste0("Stocks: ", input$strat1_stocks, "%") })
        )
      )
    )
  ),
  div(
    style = "margin-top: -5px; margin-bottom: -5px;",
    checkboxInput("enable_strat2", "Compare Strategy #2", value = TRUE)
  ),
  renderUI({
    opacity <- ifelse(input$enable_strat2, "1.0", "0.4")
    pointer <- ifelse(input$enable_strat2, "auto", "none")

    div(
      style = paste0(
        "opacity: ", opacity,
        "; pointer-events: ", pointer,
        "; transition: 0.3s; margin-top: 0px;"
      ),
      fluidRow(
        column(
          3,
          h4(HTML(paste0(
            "<span style='color:", color_wealth_strat2,
            ";'>&#9632;</span> <b>Strategy #2</b>"
          )))
        ),
        column(
          2,
          div(
            style = "text-align: right; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #7f8c8d;",
            renderText({ paste0("Bonds: ", 100 - input$strat2_stocks, "%") })
          )
        ),
        column(
          5,
          sliderInput(
            "strat2_stocks",
            NULL,
            min = 0,
            max = 100,
            value = 50,
            post = "%",
            width = "100%"
          )
        ),
        column(
          2,
          div(
            style = "text-align: left; padding-top: 5px; font-weight: bold; color: #2c3e50;",
            renderText({ paste0("Stocks: ", input$strat2_stocks, "%") })
          )
        )
      )
    )
  })
)
```

Row {data-height=750}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Utility Trajectory & Decomposition* {data-width=600}

```{r plot_utility_traj}
shiny::fillCol(
  flex = c(1, 1),
  renderPlotly({
    data <- simulation_data()
    plot_utility_trajectory(data$traj)
  }),
  renderPlotly({
    data <- simulation_data()
    plot_utility_waterfall(data$stats)
  })
)
```

### *Projected Wealth Trajectory* {data-width=400}

```{r plot_wealth_traj}
div(
  style = "text-align: right; z-index: 100; position: relative;",
  radioButtons(
    "wealth_scale",
    NULL,
    choices = c("Log" = "log10", "Linear" = "linear"),
    selected = "log10",
    inline = TRUE
  )
)

renderPlotly({
  data <- simulation_data()
  scale_val <- if (is.null(input$wealth_scale)) "log10" else input$wealth_scale
  plot_wealth_trajectory(data$traj, scale_type = scale_val)
})
```

Goal Planner {icon="fa-bullseye"}
=======================================================================

Row {data-height=170}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Planner Inputs* {data-width=750}

```{r allocation_controls}
fluidRow(
  column(
    4,
    sliderInput(
      "goal_time_horizon",
      "Time horizon",
      min = 1,
      max = 50,
      value = 30,
      step = 1,
      post = " years",
      width = "100%"
    )
  ),
  column(
    4,
    sliderInput(
      "goal_risk_aversion",
      "Risk aversion (gamma)",
      min = 0,
      max = 6,
      value = 3,
      step = 0.25,
      width = "100%"
    )
  ),
  column(
    4,
    sliderInput(
      "goal_multiplier",
      "Target purchasing power",
      min = 1,
      max = 10,
      value = 4,
      step = 0.5,
      post = "x",
      width = "100%"
    )
  )
)
```

### *View & Meaning* {data-width=250}

```{r allocation_assumptions}
radioButtons(
  "distribution_scale",
  "Wealth scale",
  choices = c("Log" = "log", "Linear" = "linear"),
  selected = "log",
  inline = TRUE
)

radioButtons(
  "distribution_range",
  "Histogram range",
  choices = c("Same scale" = "comparable", "Zoom selected" = "selected"),
  selected = "comparable",
  inline = TRUE
)

div(
  class = "assumption-card",
  strong("Wealth is inflation-adjusted."),
  br(),
  span("Same scale keeps every allocation directly comparable. Zoom selected reveals its distribution shape.")
)
```

Row {data-height=430}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Probability of Reaching the Goal* {data-width=600}

```{r goal_probability}
renderPlotly({
  summary_data <- allocation_analysis()
  settings <- planner_settings()
  selected_stocks <- selected_allocation()
  selected_data <- summary_data %>% filter(Stocks == selected_stocks)
  utility_best <- summary_data %>%
    slice_max(`Risk-adjusted utility`, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
  max_probability <- max(summary_data$`Goal probability`, na.rm = TRUE)
  probability_ceiling <- if (max_probability <= 0) {
    0.05
  } else {
    min(1, max(0.05, max_probability * 1.2))
  }

  plot_ly(
    data = summary_data,
    x = ~Stocks,
    y = ~`Goal probability`,
    type = "scatter",
    mode = "lines+markers",
    name = "Chance of reaching goal",
    line = list(color = color_wealth_strat1, width = 3),
    marker = list(size = 7, color = color_wealth_strat1),
    text = ~paste0(
      Stocks, "% stocks / ", Bonds, "% bonds<br>",
      "Goal probability: ", percent(`Goal probability`, accuracy = 0.1), "<br>",
      "Chance below start: ", percent(`Chance below start`, accuracy = 0.1), "<br>",
      "Median wealth: ", dollar(`Median wealth`)
    ),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
    add_markers(
      data = selected_data,
      x = ~Stocks,
      y = ~`Goal probability`,
      name = "Selected allocation",
      marker = list(
        size = 15,
        color = "rgba(255,255,255,0)",
        line = list(color = "#111827", width = 4)
      ),
      text = ~paste0(
        "Selected: ", Stocks, "% stocks<br>",
        "Goal probability: ", percent(`Goal probability`, accuracy = 0.1)
      ),
      hoverinfo = "text"
    ) %>%
    add_markers(
      data = utility_best,
      x = ~Stocks,
      y = ~`Goal probability`,
      name = "Highest utility",
      marker = list(size = 12, color = color_wealth_strat2, symbol = "diamond"),
      text = ~paste0(
        "Highest risk-adjusted utility<br>",
        Stocks, "% stocks / ", Bonds, "% bonds<br>",
        "Utility: ", round(`Risk-adjusted utility`, 1)
      ),
      hoverinfo = "text"
    ) %>%
    layout(
      xaxis = list(title = "Stock allocation", ticksuffix = "%"),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Probability of reaching goal",
        tickformat = ".0%",
        range = c(0, probability_ceiling)
      ),
      legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0, y = -0.2),
      margin = list(l = 90, r = 25, t = 25, b = 85),
      hovermode = "closest"
    ) %>%
    config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
})
```

### *Selected Outcome Distribution* {data-width=400}

```{r terminal_distribution}
renderPlotly({
  distribution <- selected_distribution()
  settings <- planner_settings()
  stocks <- selected_allocation()
  target_wealth <- 100 * settings$goal_multiplier
  median_wealth <- median(distribution$Wealth)
  goal_probability <- mean(distribution$Wealth >= target_wealth)
  loss_probability <- mean(distribution$Wealth < 100)
  range_mode <- if (is.null(input$distribution_range)) {
    "comparable"
  } else {
    input$distribution_range
  }

  if (range_mode == "selected") {
    display_lower <- unname(quantile(distribution$Wealth, 0.005))
    display_upper <- unname(quantile(distribution$Wealth, 0.995))
    range_caption <- "Central 99% of the selected allocation; use Same scale for direct comparison."
  } else {
    shared_range <- allocation_display_range()
    display_lower <- shared_range[["lower"]]
    display_upper <- shared_range[["upper"]]
    range_caption <- "Same central-99% wealth range for every allocation, enabling direct comparison."
  }

  if (display_upper <= display_lower * 1.001) {
    display_lower <- max(0.01, display_lower * 0.95)
    display_upper <- display_upper * 1.05
  }
  display_distribution <- distribution %>%
    filter(Wealth >= display_lower, Wealth <= display_upper)
  target_in_view <- target_wealth >= display_lower && target_wealth <= display_upper
  start_in_view <- 100 >= display_lower && 100 <= display_upper
  goal_note <- if (target_in_view) {
    paste0("Goal ", dollar(target_wealth), " shown in orange")
  } else {
    paste0("Goal ", dollar(target_wealth), " is outside the displayed central range")
  }

  p <- ggplot(display_distribution, aes(x = Wealth)) +
    geom_histogram(
      bins = 42,
      fill = color_wealth_strat1,
      color = "white",
      alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = median_wealth,
      color = color_wealth_strat2,
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(stocks, "% Stocks / ", 100 - stocks, "% Bonds"),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Reach goal: ", percent(goal_probability, accuracy = 0.1),
        " | Below start: ", percent(loss_probability, accuracy = 0.1),
        "\n", goal_note
      ),
      x = "Terminal wealth (starting wealth = $100)",
      y = "Simulated outcomes",
      caption = range_caption
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, color = "#6b7280")
    )

  if (start_in_view) {
    p <- p + geom_vline(
      xintercept = 100,
      color = "#e74c3c",
      linewidth = 0.8,
      linetype = "dashed"
    )
  }

  if (target_in_view) {
    p <- p + geom_vline(
      xintercept = target_wealth,
      color = "#f39c12",
      linewidth = 1,
      linetype = "dashed"
    )
  }

  if (input$distribution_scale == "log") {
    p <- p + scale_x_log10(
      labels = label_dollar(),
      limits = c(display_lower, display_upper)
    )
  } else {
    p <- p + scale_x_continuous(
      labels = label_dollar(),
      limits = c(display_lower, display_upper)
    )
  }

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("x", "count")) %>%
    layout(
      margin = list(l = 65, r = 20, t = 75, b = 60),
      showlegend = FALSE
    ) %>%
    config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
})
```

Row {data-height=430}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### *Interactive Allocation Decision Table*

```{r allocation_table}
renderDT({
  data <- allocation_analysis()
  default_row <- which.min(abs(data$Stocks - 60))

  datatable(
    data,
    rownames = FALSE,
    selection = list(mode = "single", selected = default_row),
    callback = JS(
      "table.on('click', 'tbody tr', function() {",
      "  var row = table.row(this).data();",
      "  if (row) {",
      "    Shiny.setInputValue('selected_allocation_pct', parseFloat(row[0]), {priority: 'event'});",
      "  }",
      "});"
    ),
    options = list(
      pageLength = 11,
      lengthChange = FALSE,
      searching = FALSE,
      scrollX = TRUE,
      order = list(list(0, "asc"))
    ),
    class = "stripe hover compact"
  ) %>%
    formatString(c("Stocks", "Bonds"), suffix = "%") %>%
    formatPercentage(c("Goal probability", "Chance below start"), digits = 1) %>%
    formatCurrency(
      c(
        "Expected wealth",
        "Median wealth",
        "Downside (2.5%)",
        "Upside (97.5%)"
      ),
      currency = "$",
      digits = 1
    ) %>%
    formatRound("Risk-adjusted utility", digits = 1)
}, server = FALSE)
```

```{r reactive_orchestration, include=FALSE}
portfolio_settings <- debounce(
  reactive({
    req(
      input$time_horizon,
      input$risk_aversion,
      input$strat1_stocks
    )
    list(
      time_horizon = input$time_horizon,
      risk_aversion = input$risk_aversion,
      strat1_stocks = input$strat1_stocks,
      enable_strat2 = input$enable_strat2,
      strat2_stocks = input$strat2_stocks
    )
  }),
  millis = 300
)

simulation_data <- reactive({
  settings <- portfolio_settings()

  months <- settings$time_horizon * 12

  matrix_1 <- generate_wealth_matrix(
    w_stocks = settings$strat1_stocks / 100,
    w_bonds = (100 - settings$strat1_stocks) / 100,
    mu_s = market_params$mu_s,
    sigma_s = market_params$sigma_s,
    mu_b = market_params$mu_b,
    df_shape = deg_free_shape,
    df_lower = deg_free_lower,
    df_upper = deg_free_upper,
    df_horizon = df_horizon,
    W0 = 100,
    months = months
  )

  df_traj_1 <- calculate_trajectory_metrics(matrix_1)
  util_1 <- calculate_utility_metrics(matrix_1, gamma = settings$risk_aversion)

  df_strat1 <- df_traj_1 %>%
    left_join(util_1$trajectory, by = "month") %>%
    mutate(Strategy = "Strategy 1")
  stats_1 <- tibble(Strategy = "Strategy 1", !!!util_1$final_stats)

  if (settings$enable_strat2) {
    req(settings$strat2_stocks)
    matrix_2 <- generate_wealth_matrix(
      w_stocks = settings$strat2_stocks / 100,
      w_bonds = (100 - settings$strat2_stocks) / 100,
      mu_s = market_params$mu_s,
      sigma_s = market_params$sigma_s,
      mu_b = market_params$mu_b,
      df_shape = deg_free_shape,
      df_lower = deg_free_lower,
      df_upper = deg_free_upper,
      df_horizon = df_horizon,
      W0 = 100,
      months = months
    )

    df_traj_2 <- calculate_trajectory_metrics(matrix_2)
    util_2 <- calculate_utility_metrics(matrix_2, gamma = settings$risk_aversion)

    df_strat2 <- df_traj_2 %>%
      left_join(util_2$trajectory, by = "month") %>%
      mutate(Strategy = "Strategy 2")
    stats_2 <- tibble(Strategy = "Strategy 2", !!!util_2$final_stats)

    df_comparison <- bind_rows(df_strat1, df_strat2)
    df_stats_comparison <- bind_rows(stats_1, stats_2)
  } else {
    df_comparison <- df_strat1
    df_stats_comparison <- stats_1
  }

  list(traj = df_comparison, stats = df_stats_comparison)
})

planner_settings <- debounce(
  reactive({
    req(input$goal_time_horizon, input$goal_risk_aversion, input$goal_multiplier)
    list(
      time_horizon = input$goal_time_horizon,
      risk_aversion = input$goal_risk_aversion,
      goal_multiplier = input$goal_multiplier
    )
  }),
  millis = 250
)

allocation_surface <- reactive({
  settings <- planner_settings()
  build_terminal_surface(
    stock_allocations = seq(0, 100, by = 5),
    months = settings$time_horizon * 12
  )
})

allocation_display_range <- reactive({
  surface_wealth <- allocation_surface()$wealth
  c(
    lower = min(apply(surface_wealth, 1, quantile, probs = 0.005)),
    upper = max(apply(surface_wealth, 1, quantile, probs = 0.995))
  )
})

allocation_analysis <- reactive({
  settings <- planner_settings()
  summarize_terminal_surface(
    surface = allocation_surface(),
    gamma = settings$risk_aversion,
    target_wealth = 100 * settings$goal_multiplier
  )
})

selected_allocation <- reactive({
  data <- allocation_analysis()
  selected_stocks <- input$selected_allocation_pct

  if (length(selected_stocks) == 1 && is.finite(selected_stocks)) {
    return(data$Stocks[which.min(abs(data$Stocks - selected_stocks))])
  }

  data$Stocks[which.min(abs(data$Stocks - 60))]
})

selected_distribution <- reactive({
  surface_distribution(allocation_surface(), selected_allocation())
})

```
